title: Memory Provenance Tagging in Soul Memory
description: Add a provenance field to every memory entry stored in the soul system: `source` (one of `observed`, `recalled`, `a2a_input`) and `hop_count` (integer, defaults to 0, incremented each time the memory transits an agent boundary). When Mira retrieves a memory to include as context in a sub-agent call or notes_outbox message, the provenance rides along. Sub-agents are instructed (via prompt) to treat `a2a_input` memories with explicit skepticism and not to re-store them at full confidence.
rationale: The reading note identifies that false memories become more entrenched than true ones through repeated retrieval, and in A2A chains this becomes contagious: one agent's high-confidence error arrives as 'reliable context' at the next agent, gets re-stored at even higher confidence, and propagates forward. The note asks whether explicit citation-style source annotation could break the self-reinforcement loop. Mira already has a soul/memory layer and passes context across agent boundaries via notes_outbox — this is exactly the propagation channel described. Without provenance, every memory looks equally authoritative regardless of whether it was directly observed or relayed through three agent hops.
diff_description: In `agents/shared/soul/memory.py`: change the memory entry schema to include two new fields — `source: Literal['observed', 'recalled', 'a2a_input'] = 'observed'` and `hop_count: int = 0`. When a memory is written from an agent's own reasoning, `source='observed'`. When a memory is retrieved and re-stored (e.g. during reflection or self-evolve), `source='recalled'`. When a memory arrives via notes_inbox or as a sub-agent return value and is about to be stored, `source='a2a_input'` and `hop_count = sender_hop_count + 1`. Add a retrieval filter: memories with `hop_count >= 2` are returned with a `[unverified chain: N hops]` prefix tag injected into the text, signaling to the consuming prompt that the claim needs independent verification before being trusted. In `agents/shared/prompts.py`: add a system-level instruction that any context block containing `[unverified chain]` tags should be treated as a hypothesis rather than a fact. In `agents/super/core.py`: when constructing context for sub-agent dispatch, serialize provenance metadata alongside memory text so it isn't silently stripped.
